methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $392.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ARC Clean Technology is a Series G nuclear energy company founded by Don Wolf that has raised $50M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector comp universe consists of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 5 private peers (Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, Valar Atomics), though the narrow peer set at ARC's stage and valuation tier remains limited.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-Progression analysis, using the $50M+ total raised as a proxy base value of $392.0B, applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G advancement, yielding $490.0B. Peer-Median methodology was not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints meeting same-stage and adjacent-stage filters within the nuclear sector—while four same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the minimum three-company threshold when filtered by stage and valuation tier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $416.5B (low) to $563.5B (high), with a median estimate of $490.0B. This range reflects a single-method triangulation and therefore carries material uncertainty. Key limitations include the sparse peer comparable set in nuclear energy at this capital stage and reliance on total raised as a proxy metric rather than direct transaction comparables. Validation against additional nuclear sector transaction data or cross-sector stage-progression analysis would strengthen conviction in this range.
